contentBlocks.original:
त्रिदलं त्रिगुणाकारं त्रिनेत्रं च त्रियायुधं ।
त्रिजन्म पापसंहारम् एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणं ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tridalaṃ triguṇākāraṃ trinetraṃ ca triyāyudhaṃ .
trijanma pāpasaṃhāram ekabilvaṃ śivārpaṇaṃ ..1..
contentBlocks.translation: The three-leaved bilva, embodying the three gunas, represents the three-eyed Lord wielding the three weapons. It destroys the sins accumulated over three births. I offer this single bilva leaf to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रि-दलम् (tri-dalaṃ) – three-leaved (tri:three | dalaṃ:leaf); त्रि-गुण-आकारम् (tri-guṇa-ākāraṃ) – having the form of three gunas (tri:three | guṇa:qualities | ākāraṃ:form); त्रि-नेत्रम् (tri-netraṃ) – three-eyed (tri:three | netraṃ:eyed); च (ca) – and (ca:and); त्रि-आयुधम् (tri-āyudhaṃ) – wielding three weapons (tri:three | āyudhaṃ:weapons); त्रि-जन्म (tri-janma) – of three births (tri:three | janma:births); पाप-संहारम् (pāpa-saṃhāram) – destroyer of sins (pāpa:sins | saṃhāram:destroyer); एक-बिल्वम् (eka-bilvaṃ) – one bilva leaf (eka:one | bilvaṃ:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇaṃ) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇaṃ:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिशाखैः बिल्वपत्रैश्च ह्यच्छिद्रैः कोमलैः शुभैः ।
शिवपूजां करिष्यामि ह्येकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥ २॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
triśākhaiḥ bilvapatraiśca hyacchidraiḥ komalaiḥ śubhaiḥ .
śivapūjāṃ kariṣyāmi hyekabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam .. 2..
contentBlocks.translation: With three-leafed bilva leaves that are whole, tender, and auspicious, I shall perform the worship of Lord Shiva. This one bilva leaf is my offering to Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रि-शाखैः (tri-śākhaiḥ) – with three branches/leaves (tri:three | śākhaiḥ:with branches); बिल्व-पत्रैः च (bilva-patraiḥ ca) – and with bilva leaves (bilva:bilva | patraiḥ:with leaves | ca:and); हि अच्छिद्रैः (hi acchidraiḥ) – indeed with unbroken/whole ones (hi:indeed | acchidraiḥ:with unbroken); कोमलैः (komalaiḥ) – with tender ones (komalaiḥ:with tender); शुभैः (śubhaiḥ) – with auspicious ones (śubhaiḥ:with auspicious); शिव-पूजाम् (śiva-pūjām) – worship of Shiva (śiva:Shiva | pūjām:worship); करिष्यामि (kariṣyāmi) – I shall perform (kariṣyāmi:I shall do); हि एक-बिल्वम् (hi eka-bilvam) – indeed one bilva leaf (hi:indeed | eka:one | bilvam:bilva); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
अखण्ड बिल्वपत्रेण पूजिते नन्दिकेश्वरे ।
शुद्ध्यन्ति सर्वपापेभ्यो ह्येकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
akhaṇḍa bilvapatreṇa pūjite nandikeśvare .
śuddhyanti sarvapāpebhyo hyekabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ..3..
contentBlocks.translation: When Nandikeshvara, the divine bull and guardian of Shiva, is worshipped with an unbroken bilva leaf, all sins are purified. Thus, the offering of a single bilva leaf to Shiva grants complete absolution from all transgressions.
contentBlocks.meanings: अखण्ड (akhaṇḍa) – unbroken, whole (akhaṇḍa:unbroken); बिल्व-पत्रेण (bilva-patreṇa) – with a bilva leaf (bilva:bilva tree | patreṇa:with leaf); पूजिते (pūjite) – when worshipped (pūjite:when worshipped); नन्दि-केश्वरे (nandi-keśvare) – in Nandikeshvara, lord of Nandi (nandi:Nandi | keśvare:in the lord); शुद्ध्यन्ति (śuddhyanti) – they are purified (śuddhyanti:they become pure); सर्व-पापेभ्यः (sarva-pāpebhyaḥ) – from all sins (sarva:all | pāpebhyaḥ:from sins); हि (hi) – indeed, verily (hi:indeed); एक-बिल्वम् (eka-bilvam) – one bilva leaf (eka:one | bilvam:bilva); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
शालिग्राम शिलामेकां विप्राणां जातु चार्पयेत् ।
सोमयज्ञ महापुण्यं एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāligrāma śilāmekāṃ viprāṇāṃ jātu cārpayet .
somayajña mahāpuṇyaṃ ekabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam .. 4..
contentBlocks.translation: Offering a single Shaligrama stone to the brahmins bestows merit equal to the great Soma sacrifice. Similarly, offering a single bilva leaf to Lord Shiva yields tremendous spiritual merit.
contentBlocks.meanings: शालिग्राम-शिलाम् (śāligrāma-śilām) – Shaligrama stone (śāligrāma:Shaligrama | śilām:stone); एकाम् (ekām) – one (ekām:one); विप्राणाम् (viprāṇām) – to the brahmins (viprāṇām:to the brahmins); जातु (jātu) – ever, at any time (jātu:ever); च अर्पयेत् (ca arpayet) – and should offer (ca:and | arpayet:should offer); सोम-यज्ञ (soma-yajña) – Soma sacrifice (soma:Soma | yajña:sacrifice); महा-पुण्यम् (mahā-puṇyam) – great merit (mahā:great | puṇyam:merit); एक-बिल्वम् (eka-bilvam) – one bilva leaf (eka:one | bilvam:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
दन्तिकोटि सहस्राणी वाजपेय शतानि च ।
कोटिकन्या महादानं एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dantikoṭi sahasrāṇī vājapeya śatāni ca .
koṭikanyā mahādānaṃ ekabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ..5..
contentBlocks.translation: Ten million elephants given in charity, hundreds of Vajapeya sacrifices performed, the great gift of ten million maidens in marriage — all these are equaled by offering a single bilva leaf to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: दन्ति-कोटि (danti-koṭi) – ten million elephants (danti:elephant | koṭi:ten million); सहस्राणि (sahasrāṇī) – thousands (sahasrāṇī:thousands); वाजपेय (vājapeya) – Vajapeya sacrifice (vājapeya:a type of Vedic sacrifice); शतानि (śatāni) – hundreds (śatāni:hundreds); च (ca) – and (ca:and); कोटि-कन्या (koṭi-kanyā) – ten million maidens (koṭi:ten million | kanyā:maiden/daughter); महा-दानम् (mahā-dānam) – great gift (mahā:great | dānam:gift/charity); एक-बिल्वम् (eka-bilvam) – one bilva leaf (eka:one | bilvam:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्म्यास्तनुत उत्पन्नं महादेवस्य च प्रियम् ।
बिल्ववृक्षं प्रयच्छामि ह्येकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmyāstanuta utpannaṃ mahādevasya ca priyam .
bilvavṛkṣaṃ prayacchāmi hyekabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam ..6..
contentBlocks.translation: Born from the body of Lakshmi and beloved of Mahadeva, I offer the bilva tree. Even a single bilva leaf becomes an offering to Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: लक्ष्म्याः-तनुतः (lakṣmyāḥ-tanutaḥ) – from the body of Lakshmi (lakṣmyāḥ:of Lakshmi | tanutaḥ:from the body); उत्पन्नम् (utpannam) – born, arisen (utpannam:born); महादेवस्य (mahādevasya) – of Mahadeva (mahādevasya:of the Great God); च प्रियम् (ca priyam) – and beloved (ca:and | priyam:beloved); बिल्व-वृक्षम् (bilva-vṛkṣam) – the bilva tree (bilva:bilva | vṛkṣam:tree); प्रयच्छामि (prayacchāmi) – I offer (prayacchāmi:I offer); हि एक-बिल्वम् (hi eka-bilvam) – indeed a single bilva leaf (hi:indeed | eka:one | bilvam:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
दर्शनं बिल्ववृक्षस्य स्पर्शनं पापनाशनम् ।
अघोरपापसंहारं एकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥ ७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
darśanaṃ bilvavṛkṣasya sparśanaṃ pāpanāśanam .
aghorapāpasaṃhāraṃ ekabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam .. 7..
contentBlocks.translation: The mere sight of the bilva tree is sacred, and touching it destroys all sins. That which annihilates even the most terrible sins — I offer one bilva leaf to Lord Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: दर्शनम् (darśanam) – the sight of (darśanam:sight); बिल्व-वृक्षस्य (bilva-vṛkṣasya) – of the bilva tree (bilva:bilva | vṛkṣasya:of the tree); स्पर्शनम् (sparśanam) – the touch (sparśanam:touch); पाप-नाशनम् (pāpa-nāśanam) – destroyer of sins (pāpa:sin | nāśanam:destroyer); अघोर-पाप-संहारम् (aghora-pāpa-saṃhāram) – destruction of terrible sins (aghora:terrible | pāpa:sin | saṃhāram:destruction); एक-बिल्वम् (eka-bilvam) – one bilva leaf (eka:one | bilvam:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
मूलतो ब्रह्मरूपाय मध्यतो विष्णुरूपिणे ।
अग्रतः शिवरूपाय ह्येकबिल्वं शिवार्पणम् ॥ ८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mūlato brahmarūpāya madhyato viṣṇurūpiṇe .
agrataḥ śivarūpāya hyekabilvaṃ śivārpaṇam .. 8..
contentBlocks.translation: To the one who is Brahma in form at the root, to the one who bears the form of Vishnu in the middle, and to the one who assumes the form of Shiva at the tip — I offer this single bilva leaf to Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: मूलतः (mūlataḥ) – at the root (mūlataḥ:at the root); ब्रह्म-रूपाय (brahma-rūpāya) – to the one of Brahma form (brahma:Brahma | rūpāya:to the form); मध्यतः (madhyataḥ) – in the middle (madhyataḥ:in the middle); विष्णु-रूपिणे (viṣṇu-rūpiṇe) – to the one bearing Vishnu's form (viṣṇu:Vishnu | rūpiṇe:to the one having form); अग्रतः (agrataḥ) – at the tip/front (agrataḥ:at the tip); शिव-रूपाय (śiva-rūpāya) – to the one of Shiva form (śiva:Shiva | rūpāya:to the form); हि (hi) – indeed (hi:indeed); एक-बिल्वम् (eka-bilvam) – one bilva leaf (eka:one | bilvam:bilva leaf); शिव-अर्पणम् (śiva-arpaṇam) – offering to Shiva (śiva:Shiva | arpaṇam:offering);

contentBlocks.original:
बिल्वाष्टकमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेत् शिवसन्निधौ ।
सर्वपाप विनिर्मुक्तः शिवलोकमवाप्नुयात् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bilvāṣṭakamidaṃ puṇyaṃ yaḥ paṭhet śivasannidhau .
sarvapāpa vinirmuktaḥ śivalokamavāpnuyāt ..
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever recites this sacred Bilvashtakam in the presence of Lord Shiva, becomes completely freed from all sins and attains the abode of Shiva.
contentBlocks.meanings: बिल्व-अष्टकम् (bilva-aṣṭakam) – the eight verses on Bilva (bilva:Bilva leaf | aṣṭakam:eight verses); इदम् (idam) – this (idam:this); पुण्यम् (puṇyam) – sacred, meritorious (puṇyam:sacred); यः (yaḥ) – whoever (yaḥ:whoever); पठेत् (paṭhet) – recites (paṭhet:recites); शिव-सन्निधौ (śiva-sannidhau) – in the presence of Shiva (śiva:Shiva | sannidhau:in the presence of); सर्व-पाप (sarva-pāpa) – all sins (sarva:all | pāpa:sins); विनिर्मुक्तः (vinirmuktaḥ) – completely freed (vinirmuktaḥ:completely freed); शिव-लोकम् (śiva-lokam) – the abode of Shiva (śiva:Shiva | lokam:abode); अवाप्नुयात् (avāpnuyāt) – attains (avāpnuyāt:attains);
